slides.title: Observability That Pays for Itself
slides.subtitle: Turning raw telemetry into faster incidents, cheaper bills, and calmer on-call

slides.title: Where the time and money go today

stats.label: Log spend growth
stats.caption: year over year, mostly debug noise

slides.title: What good observability actually gives you

items.heading: Find problems faster
items.body: Correlated traces, logs, and metrics in one click instead of five tabs.

items.heading: Alerts you trust
items.body: Symptom-based pages tied to SLOs, not noisy host-level thresholds.

items.heading: Lower telemetry bills
items.body: Sample and tier data by value so debug noise stops driving cost.

items.heading: Safer releases
items.body: Catch regressions in deploy windows before customers feel them.

slides.title: How we roll it out in 90 days

steps.label: Define SLOs
steps.detail: Agree error budgets with each product team.

steps.label: Re-tier data
steps.detail: Sample, route, and drop low-value telemetry.

steps.label: Rewire alerts
steps.detail: Replace host alerts with SLO burn pages.

steps.label: Measure
steps.detail: Track MTTD, spend, and page volume monthly.

slides.title: Pick the rollout that fits your risk appetite

cards.heading: Pilot
cards.body: One squad, one critical service. Prove MTTD and cost wins in six weeks with zero platform-wide commitment.

cards.heading: Standard
cards.body: A full domain adopts shared instrumentation, SLOs, and golden dashboards. The recommended path for most teams.

cards.heading: Enterprise
cards.body: Org-wide rollout with centralized governance, data tiering policy, and an internal enablement program.

slides.title: Let's instrument your first service this sprint
